lines.english: you shall have to leave it all behind.
to one who takes the Lord's Name. ||1||
These things seem like only a dream,
Forsaking the Lord, and clinging to another,
O Nanak, becomes His devotee. ||2||7||163||232||
continue to live.
they run toward death and reincarnation.
But those humble beings, who attach themselves to the Lord, Har, Har,
One who is blessed with the Lord's Mercy,

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਰਥਾ ਕਹਉ ਕਉਨ ਸਿਉ ਮਨ ਕੀ ॥
lines.translit: birathaa khau kaun siau man kee |
lines.english: Who should I tell the condition of the mind?
Raag Aasaa, Ninth Mehl:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਭਿ ਗ੍ਰਸਿਓ ਦਸ ਹੂ ਦਿਸ ਧਾਵਤ ਆਸਾ ਲਾਗਿਓ ਧਨ ਕੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: lobh grasio das hoo dis dhaavat aasaa laagio dhan kee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Engrossed in greed, running around in the ten directions, you hold to your hopes of wealth. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖ ਕੈ ਹੇਤਿ ਬਹੁਤੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਪਾਵਤ ਸੇਵ ਕਰਤ ਜਨ ਜਨ ਕੀ ॥
lines.translit: sukh kai het bahut dukh paavat sev karat jan jan kee |
lines.english: For the sake of pleasure, you suffer such great pain, and you have to serve each and every person.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਆਰਹਿ ਦੁਆਰਿ ਸੁਆਨ ਜਿਉ ਡੋਲਤ ਨਹ ਸੁਧ ਰਾਮ ਭਜਨ ਕੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: duaareh duaar suaan jiau ddolat neh sudh raam bhajan kee |1|
lines.english: You wander from door to door like a dog, unconscious of the Lord's meditation. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਅਕਾਰਥ ਖੋਵਤ ਲਾਜ ਨ ਲੋਕ ਹਸਨ ਕੀ ॥
lines.translit: maanas janam akaarath khovat laaj na lok hasan kee |
lines.english: You lose this human life in vain, and You are not even ashamed when others laugh at you.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਕਿਉ ਨਹੀ ਗਾਵਤ ਕੁਮਤਿ ਬਿਨਾਸੈ ਤਨ ਕੀ ॥੨॥੧॥੨੩੩॥
lines.translit: naanak har jas kiau nahee gaavat kumat binaasai tan kee |2|1|233|
lines.english: O Nanak, why not sing the Lord's Praises, so that you may be rid of the body's evil disposition? ||2||1||233||
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Raag Aasaa, First Mehl, Ashtpadheeyaa, Second House:
Like water vapor in the sky, he remains absorbed in the Lord.
He descends the treacherous precipice, to bathe in the cleansing pool;

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਸੁ ਸਤੁ ਝੋਲਿ ਮਹਾ ਰਸੁ ਪਾਵੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: ras sat jhol mahaa ras paavai |1|
lines.english: He churns the true pleasures to obtain the supreme nectar. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਕੈ ਨ ਬੋਲੈ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: bakai na bolai har gun gaavai |
lines.english: without speaking or saying anything, he sings the Glorious Praises of the Lord.
Listen to such spiritual wisdom, O my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਰਿਪੁਰਿ ਧਾਰਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਸਭ ਠਉਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: bharipur dhaar rahiaa sabh tthaure |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The Lord is totally pervading and permeating all places. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਬ੍ਰਤੁ ਨੇਮੁ ਨ ਕਾਲੁ ਸੰਤਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: sach brat nem na kaal santaavai |
lines.english: One who makes Truthfulness his fast and religious vows, does not suffer the pain of death.
He dwells in the Tenth Gate, immersed in the Samaadhi of deep meditation.
Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, he burns away his anger.
Touching the philosopher's stone, he obtains the supreme status. ||2||
For the benefit of the mind, churn the true essence of reality;
bathing in the over-flowing tank of nectar, filth is washed away.
We become like the One with whom we are imbued.
Whatever the Creator does, comes to pass. ||3||
Smear your body with the ashes of dedicated service,
The Guru is cool and soothing like ice; He puts out the fire of the mind.
Let the Immaculate Bani of the Word be your playing of the flute. ||4||
Spiritual wisdom within is the supreme, sublime nectar.
and live in the home of peace - make this your religious order.
Worship and adoration within is the Lord's dwelling.
He is the One who blends one's light with the Divine Light. ||5||
Contemplation of the Guru is one's bathing at holy places of pilgrimage.
He delights in the delightful wisdom of loving the One Lord.
He performs his works in obedience to the Will of his Lord and Master.
He is one of the self-elect - he merges with the Lord, who occupies the throne.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਲ ਮਹਿ ਉਪਜੈ ਜਲ ਤੇ ਦੂਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: jal meh upajai jal te door |
lines.english: The lotus originates in the water, and yet it remains distinct from the water.
Just so, the Divine Light pervades and permeates the water of the world.
The Unknowable Lord cannot be understood. ||6||
Inwardly and outwardly, there is none other than Him.
I sing the Glories of the Lord, the treasure of virtue; I behold Him ever-present. ||7||
Who is near, and who is far away?
